title: Mid session 2901 T224 B
body:
NAME OF CANDIDATE: . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . .
                               STUDENT NUMBER: . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . .



      THE UNIVERSITY OF NEW SOUTH WALES

      SCHOOL OF MATHEMATICS AND STATISTICS


                                 Term 2, 2024


                              MATH2901
              Higher Theory of Statistics
                  Mid-session test B




 (1) TIME ALLOWED – 50 minutes

 (2) TOTAL NUMBER OF QUESTIONS – 3

 (3) ANSWER ALL QUESTIONS

 (4) THE QUESTIONS ARE OF EQUAL VALUE

 (5) THIS PAPER MAY NOT BE RETAINED BY THE CANDIDATE

 (6) Write your answers on the test paper in space provided.
     You may use your own calculators.
     You are allowed a one-sided (A4) handwritten formula sheet of your own. Ask
     if you need more paper.




All answers must be written in ink. Except where they are expressly required pencils
may only be used for drawing, sketching or graphical work.
 Term 2, 2024                           MATH2901                                Page 2


 1. [10 marks] A fair die is tossed n times independently. Let X be the number
    of 1’s and 2’s obtained, and Y be the number of 6’s obtained over the n tosses.

      a) [8 marks] Find the covariance between X and Y .
         (Hint: Write X = ni=1 Xi and Y = nj=1 Yj , where Xi = 1I{1 or 2 at the ith toss}
                              P            P
         and Yj = 1I{6 at the j th toss} )




      b) [2 marks] The covariance that you obtain in a) should be negative.
         Explain intuitively why.




                                                                 Please see over . . .
 Term 2, 2024                             MATH2901                                  Page 3


 2. [10 marks] Let (X1 , X2 ) be a bivariate discrete vector, with SX1 = {1, 2, 3},
    SX2 = {1, 2, 3} and pX1 X2 (x1 , x2 ) = cx1 x2 for (x1 , x2 ) ∈ SX1 X2 = {(x1 , x2 ) ∈
    SX1 × SX2 : x1 ≤ x2 } and a constant c > 0.

      a) [3 marks] Determine the normalisation constant c.




      b) [2 marks] Are X1 and X2 independent? Justify.




                                                                     Please see over . . .
 Term 2, 2024                        MATH2901                 Page 4


      c) [3 marks] Find E(X1 ) and E(X2 ).




      d) [2 marks] Find Cov(X1 , X2 ).




                                               Please see over . . .
 Term 2, 2024                         MATH2901                              Page 5


 3. [10 marks] We have a computer program consisting of n = 100 pages of code.
    Let Xi be the number of errors on the ith page of code. Suppose that the Xi ’s
    are Poisson random variables with mean 1 and that they are independent.

      a) [8 marks] Call S100 = 100
                                P
                                  i=1 Xi the total number of errors over the 100
         pages. Provide an approximation for the probability P(80 ≤ S100 ≤ 90).
         (Hint: Φ(0.8) = 0.7881, Φ(0.9) = 0.8159, Φ(0.95) = 0.8289, Φ(1) =
         0.8413, Φ(2) = 0.9772, Φ(2.05) = 0.9798)




      b) [2 marks] Do you think that the previous approximation is accurate?
         Explain briefly.
